title: Evangelion Villains
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Villains             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Thu Oct 18, 2007 1:04 pm I'm a huge fan of villains, they are usually the most interesting characters to me. I think that's one of my main draws to Evangelion. There is no exact villain to the show, it almost seems to be an "eye of the beholder" kind of thing. Gendo gets blamed pretty regularly as the villain, but I don't see him as one. I see him as a heroic, if not misguided, character. Seele are also good scapegoats, but even they have noble, yet once again seemingly misguided, ambitions. Finally, there are the Angels, who only really just seem to want to live and are no more villainous than the humans who fight for the same reason. Who would you consider to be the "villain" of Evangelion? Also, is there any one character who does seem to be defined as "evil"? The closest thing to a villain I can come up with would be Keel, but I still would not consider him to be evil.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Thu Oct 18, 2007 1:07 pm I don't think there are villains in Evangelion, really. I remember reading a post on another forum in which the poster said that SEELE were actually heroes. It's very much an "eye of the beholder" situation!

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Thu Oct 18, 2007 1:35 pm Hm. Although I agree that it's open to interpretation somewhat, I've always felt SEELE to be the villain. But that's just my view.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Oct 18, 2007 1:50 pm You are the villain. You watch the suffering and do nothing. In fact you savor it; it makes for a cartoon that you can enjoy again and again. For shame, for shame.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Thu Oct 18, 2007 2:32 pm

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Thu Oct 18, 2007 2:35 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: You are the villain. You watch the suffering and do nothing. In fact you savor it; it makes for a cartoon that you can enjoy again and again. For shame, for shame.

[]:             Quote Post by Squiggy » Thu Oct 18, 2007 2:36 pm The main conflict in Evangelion wasn't with the angels but with the characters themselves. In a cheesy sort of way Shinji was his own worst enemy. That's how I see it at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Oct 18, 2007 2:44 pm Indigo Arcangel wrote: Hexon.Arq wrote: You are the villain. You watch the suffering and do nothing. In fact you savor it; it makes for a cartoon that you can enjoy again and again. For shame, for shame.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Thu Oct 18, 2007 3:18 pm If anything SEELE are the villains, too bad they're shallow characters with close to no development.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Thu Oct 18, 2007 4:13 pm I don't think you can really call SEELE villains. They are not really doing what they are doing for wholly selfish reasons, they think they are trying to help the world. Gendo's goal is selfish, and he treats Shinji appallingly (regardless of his reasons), so I suppose he's the closest you'll get to a traditional villain in Eva. MaggotMaster wrote: If anything SEELE are the villains, too bad they're shallow characters with close to no development.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Thu Oct 18, 2007 5:41 pm CitizenGeek wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: If anything SEELE are the villains, too bad they're shallow characters with close to no development.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Oct 18, 2007 5:49 pm CitizenGeek wrote: They are not really doing what they are doing for wholly selfish reasons

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 18, 2007 6:29 pm plenty of rehashing here: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=403 http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=777 http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=703 and a little more here: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=2512

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 18, 2007 7:13 pm Personally, I feel SEELE are the villains, seeing as they want to kill off the whole freaking world with their batshit crazy religion because, hey, Forced Evolution and not having Free Will is the way to go, amirite? :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Oct 18, 2007 7:51 pm Despair and suicide are the villains of our piece. Their nefarious henchmen of misunderstanding and poor communication work their evil throughout the show. Our unsung hero is hope, championed by Yui, but rarely seen in action.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Oct 18, 2007 7:52 pm every main character is their own worst enemy

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Thu Oct 18, 2007 10:02 pm Yeah SEELE are the demons, and then Kaworu was a zombie, etc, but I think MM's right in that the villains themselves weren't very charismatic, but I don't think that's very important, because this isn't really a "good vs. evil" story at all to begin with.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Oct 19, 2007 2:52 am The MP Eva Series, built through SEELE's own misguided intentions, they are essentially tools for evil, not to mention how they took pleasure in tearing apart a helpless opponent (Asuka). 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Fri Oct 19, 2007 6:02 am Personally, SEELE always seemed to be the villains to me. However, a couple people bring up a good point about the main characters being their own worst enemies. Shinji is his own worse enemy and I really can't describe it, but I think the characters are their own worst enemies.

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Fri Oct 19, 2007 7:12 am Ornette wrote: plenty of rehashing here: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=403 http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=777 http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=703 and a little more here: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=2512
